strengths: Clear identification strategy with staggered DiD design
Rich policy background linking NIDZs to entrepreneurship theory

issues.title: Unclear variable construction for EA index
issues.description: The key dependent variable (Entrepreneurial Activity index) lacks methodological details. Readers cannot assess measurement validity or replicate the construction.
issues.suggestion: Provide exact formula: EA_it = new_registrations_it / (population_it / 10000). Document outlier handling and normalization.
issues.action_rationale: Requires author decision about variable construction details — agent cannot invent methodology

issues.title: Overclaim: 'first to systematically test'

location.quote: this paper is the first to systematically test conditional heterogeneity in NIDZ effects

issues.description: The claim of being 'the first' is strong and not substantiated with a systematic literature gap analysis.
issues.suggestion: Temper to 'among the first' or provide specific comparisons showing no prior work has tested conditional heterogeneity.
issues.action_rationale: Touches core contribution claim — requires author confirmation before modifying

issues.title: Parallel trends assumption unverified

location.quote: we employ a staggered difference-in-differences design

issues.description: No pre-treatment trend visualization or formal test (e.g., event study coefficients) provided for the parallel trends assumption.
issues.suggestion: Add event-study plot showing pre-treatment coefficients are jointly insignificant (p>0.05).
issues.action_rationale: Requires new empirical analysis — agent cannot generate data

issues.title: Hedging undermines key finding

location.quote: the results seem to suggest that NIDZs might potentially have some impact

issues.description: Excessive hedging weakens an otherwise statistically significant finding (p<0.01). This reads as uncertainty where the data supports confidence.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: 'NIDZs increase regional entrepreneurial activity by 0.32 SD (p<0.01).'
issues.action_rationale: Clear presentation issue with unambiguous fix direction

issues.title: Passive voice overuse in abstract

location.quote: it is found that... it is shown that... it is argued that...

issues.description: Three consecutive passive constructions make the abstract feel impersonal and AI-generated.
issues.suggestion: Convert to active: 'We find that NIDZs increase... Our analysis shows... We argue...'
issues.action_rationale: Standard style improvement with clear direction

issues.title: Missing robustness check for spatial spillover

location.quote: neighboring regions show increased entrepreneurial activity

issues.description: Spatial spillover is claimed but no spatial econometric model (e.g., SAR, SEM) or distance-decay test is provided.
issues.suggestion: Add Spatial Durbin Model or at minimum test coefficient decay at 100km/200km/500km radii.

issues.title: Inconsistent significance reporting

location.quote: significant at the 1% level... marginally significant

issues.description: Mixed use of p-value thresholds and verbal significance descriptions. Some tables use *** notation while text uses verbal description.
issues.suggestion: Standardize: use *** p<0.01, ** p<0.05, * p<0.10 in all tables; match in text.

issues.title: Conclusion repeats results without synthesis

location.quote: this paper found that NIDZs increase entrepreneurial activity

issues.description: Conclusion merely restates findings without synthesizing theoretical implications or discussing boundary conditions.
issues.suggestion: Add: (1) what this means for NIDZ theory, (2) boundary conditions, (3) specific policy recommendations with magnitudes.
issues.action_rationale: Restructuring conclusion touches author's theoretical narrative

issues.title: Grammar: subject-verb agreement

location.quote: the literature on innovation zones show that

issues.suggestion: Change to: 'the literature on innovation zones shows that'

issues.title: Citation format inconsistency
issues.description: Mixed citation formats: some inline without parentheses, some with. Should follow journal style consistently.
issues.suggestion: Standardize all to '(Author et al., Year)' format per journal requirements.

revision_roadmap: Phase 1 (auto_fix): ISS-004, ISS-005, ISS-007, ISS-009, ISS-010 — presentation cleanup
Phase 3 (guidance): ISS-001, ISS-003, ISS-006 — new empirical work needed
